adjustment_convention_precommit_required.controller_advisory_for_s14_d1_cash_equi: Split-only is likely more appropriate for RSI mean-reversion mechanics (preserves the actual price jumps that signal mechanic responds to); dividend jumps on ex-div date are small relative to typical price moves on AAPL/MSFT/NVDA so should not materially affect signal generation. Operator final decision at SEAL.

implications.split_only: Closer to actual traded prices; dividends appear as instant downward jumps on ex-div date; bid-ask spread + slippage more reliable; commonly used for short-term trading strategies
implications.split_plus_dividend: Smooth total-return series; no dividend jumps; commonly used for long-term performance research; can mask dividend yield contribution in strategy edge

adjustment_convention_precommit_required.mixing: FORBIDDEN (entire history must use single convention; cannot mix split-only for one symbol and split+dividend for another)
options: split-only adjusted (raw close × split factor; dividends NOT adjusted into price)
split+dividend adjusted (total-return convention; dividends + splits both adjusted into price)
adjustment_convention_precommit_required.switch_post_seal: FORBIDDEN (cannot switch convention post-SEAL; would alter signal generation on dividend dates)

authorization_phrase: Authorize s14-d1-cash-equity multi-name OHLCV availability probe + DR9 audit framework only.

contingency_paths.next_authorization_at_separate_turn: Authorize s14-d1-cash-equity Tier-N spec DRAFT only — bound by DR10 v2.

contingency_paths.next_authorization_at_separate_turn: Authorize s14-d1-cash-equity shrunk-basket Tier-N spec PLAN only — bound by DR10 v2.
contingency_paths.note: Fresh candidate_record_id required (e.g., s14-d1-aapl-msft-cash-equity-... if NVDA fails). Substitution forbidden.

contingency_paths.description: Two or more fail. Single-name K9 OOS borderline-fails (~25-35/y < 50/y); revert to single-name-with-explicit-K9-borderline-acceptance OR pivot to different universe.
contingency_paths.next_authorization_at_separate_turn: Authorize s14-d1-cash-equity universe revision to alternative shortlist — bound by DR10 v2.

contingency_paths.description: DR9 returns ambiguous outcome on any symbol.
contingency_paths.next_authorization_at_separate_turn: Operator manual review + decision; no auto-progression.

contingency_paths.description: Vendor API down / quota exceeded / network error. No partial data accepted.
contingency_paths.next_authorization_at_separate_turn: Operator retries with different vendor or same vendor at later time; this RUN_BOOK remains the reference framework.

controller_advisory_vendor_preference.primary_rationale: Free tier includes full historical depth (no s14-d1 5y window blocker); both adjusted and unadjusted columns in single response (operator can defer adjustment-convention choice to SEAL); generous rate limits for 3-name × 1 historical fetch; well-respected data quality reputation
controller_advisory_vendor_preference.secondary_rationale: If operator wants zero-setup (no API key registration); admissible for diagnostic-only research with sha256 pinning at fetch time; quality adequate for modern (post-2010) history; understood quality variability is the trade-off
controller_advisory_vendor_preference.tertiary_rationale: Alpha Vantage admissible but verify current free-tier adjusted-endpoint availability; Polygon paid required if operator already has it; other paid vendors overkill for diagnostic scale

adaptations_from_futures_to_cash_equity: gap_continuity: unchanged (rows present per expected NYSE trading day; calendar = NYSE/NASDAQ US equity, NOT CME Globex)
roll_event_count: NOT_APPLICABLE_CASH_EQUITY (cash equity does not have futures rolls; this branch structurally absent for s14-d1-cash-equity)
quality_violation_count: unchanged (OHLC sanity + ts monotonic + no duplicates)
NEW: documented_split_event_consistency: vendor must apply known splits per chosen adjustment convention (load-bearing for NVDA 2024-06-10 10:1 split)
OPTIONAL/INFORMATIONAL: dividend_event_count (tracked for audit but not a DR9 fail condition unless adjustment convention chosen as split+dividend and vendor misapplies)

audit_result_schema_per_instrument.dr9_failure_branches_if_any: array of branch names that failed

per_instrument_check_procedure: Step 1: Verify the captured CSV exists at the expected path
Step 2: Verify CSV sha256 against the recorded value in the operator-captured fetch manifest
Step 3: Parse CSV; verify schema matches the manifest-declared schema (date, open, high, low, close, volume, optional adjusted_close)

Step 4: Determine NYSE/NASDAQ trading day count between first_ts and last_ts using a public US equity trading calendar (avoid weekend + US holidays; the NYSE calendar is widely available in pandas_market_calendars or similar libraries)
Step 5: Compute gap_continuity = rows_present / expected_trading_days. Threshold: >= 0.95
Step 6: Compute max_gap_ratio = max(consecutive missing trading days) / total trading days. Threshold: <= 0.30
Step 7: Count quality_violation_count = total checks failed across (OHLC sanity: low<=open<=high AND low<=close<=high; volume>=0; ts_event monotonic; no duplicate ts_event). Threshold: <= 5

Step 8: Verify documented_split_event_consistency: for each known split in the window (AAPL 2020-08-31 4:1; NVDA 2021-07-20 4:1; NVDA 2024-06-10 10:1; MSFT none) check that the CSV's price series shows EITHER (a) a clean ~25% jump (4:1 split) / ~10% (10:1 split) at the ex-split date (if unadjusted) OR (b) NO discontinuity at the ex-split date (if adjusted). Mixed/partial application FAILS this check.
Step 9: Aggregate: per-instrument DR9 status = PASS if ALL applicable thresholds met; FAIL otherwise. INCONCLUSIVE_HOLD if any threshold is ambiguous.

explicit_exclusions_this_turn: No vendor API call by controller (no yfinance call, no Polygon call, no Tiingo call, no Alpha Vantage call, no paid-vendor call)
No DATABENTO_API_KEY or any other API-key access
No DR9 audit RESULTS sealed (only the framework is pre-authored; results require operator-captured CSVs at a future turn)
No SEAL (this RUN_BOOK is not a sealed artifact)
No modification of any existing sealed artifact
filename_convention_note: <adj_convention> in filename = 'split_only' OR 'split_plus_dividend' (matches the precommitted convention at SEAL)

splits_applied_by_vendor (bool; controller verifies at audit turn)

vendor (= operator-selected vendor name from {yfinance, polygon, tiingo, alpha_vantage, paid:<name>})
boundaries_held (per-fetch attestations; same structure as s10-d1 manifest)

options: Paid vendor (Polygon paid / IEX Cloud / Refinitiv / Bloomberg)

report_kind: RUN_BOOK / vendor comparison + operator-side fetch instructions + pre-authored DR9 audit framework for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA cash-equity OHLCV. NOT a sealed artifact. Status PENDING operator-side vendor selection + fetch.
scope_of_this_run_book: Controller-side vendor comparison: yfinance / Polygon (free + paid) / Tiingo / Alpha Vantage / paid vendor — admissibility + trade-offs analysis
Pre-authored OHLCV fetch requirements: dataset semantics, schema, adjustment convention precommit, window parameters
Expected output paths + manifest schema (analogous to s10-d1 fetch manifest model)
Pre-authored DR9 audit framework adapted for cash equity (no continuous-stitch; splits/dividends instead; NYSE/NASDAQ trading calendar)
Per-symbol risk assessment: AAPL/MSFT/NVDA corporate actions in window (notably NVDA 10:1 split 2024-06-10 is load-bearing)
Contingency tree per s14-d1-cash-equity PLAN §3.2 (vendor failure / DR9 failure / partial data)
Future result-sealing scope: separate authorization required for sealing the audit result after operator-side fetch completes

title: s14-d1-cash-equity availability probe + DR9 audit RUN_BOOK (NOT a SEAL; controller-side vendor comparison + pre-authored DR9 audit framework + operator-side fetch instructions for AAPL/MSFT/NVDA)

vendor_comparison.adjustment_convention_default: split+dividend adjusted (yfinance default); unadjusted available via auto_adjust=False
cons: Quality variability is the main concern; recommend sha256 of fetched CSV pinned at fetch time to detect future drift
Yahoo Finance is unofficial/scraping-based; could break without notice
vendor_comparison.controller_advisory_recommendation: ADMISSIBLE for diagnostic-only research; precommit adjustment convention + pin sha256 at fetch time to detect future drift
vendor_comparison.data_coverage: AAPL/MSFT/NVDA: full history back to IPO (AAPL 1980, MSFT 1986, NVDA 1999); daily OHLCV reliable on major equities
vendor_comparison.free_tier_limits: no formal limit but rate-limited / can be throttled by Yahoo; recommended < 1 req/sec
known_quality_issues: Historical data quality varies; documented edge cases (e.g., stock splits in early years sometimes mis-applied)
Yahoo can serve different historical values across requests (rare but documented)
No SLA; rate-limit changes without notice
pros: Easiest install (`pip install yfinance`); minimal setup
Adequate quality for most modern history (post-2010 generally clean)

vendor_comparison.adjustment_convention_default: configurable: adjusted=true/false query parameter; defaults to adjusted
cons: Free tier 2y history limit is INSUFFICIENT for our 5y IS window (2019-01-02 to 2023-12-29)
Paid tier required for full s14-d1 window — operator-cost decision
5 calls/min rate limit OK for 3 names × 1 historical fetch
vendor_comparison.controller_advisory_recommendation: EXCELLENT QUALITY but free tier insufficient for s14-d1's 5y window; admissible only if operator authorizes paid tier OR shortens IS window to ~2y (which would weaken K9 IS margin)
vendor_comparison.data_coverage: AAPL/MSFT/NVDA: 30+ year history on paid tier; 2y on free tier (LIMITING for our 5y IS window requirement)
vendor_comparison.free_tier_limits: 5 calls/min on free tier; 2y of historical data on free tier (insufficient for s14-d1 5y IS window — NEEDS PAID TIER)
known_quality_issues: Generally high quality; data provided by official exchanges
Free tier 2y limit is the main blocker for our 5y IS window
pros: High quality reference data (official exchange aggregation)
vendor_comparison.type: REST API; official broker data aggregator

vendor_comparison.adjustment_convention_default: configurable: adjClose vs close columns; both available in single response
cons: Requires free API key registration (operator-side; controller doesn't access)
Smaller community than yfinance — less troubleshooting context
vendor_comparison.controller_advisory_recommendation: RECOMMENDED PRIMARY for s14-d1-cash-equity: high quality + full historical depth on free tier + both adjustment conventions in single fetch
vendor_comparison.data_coverage: AAPL/MSFT/NVDA: full history back to IPO; well-known clean historical record
vendor_comparison.free_tier_limits: 500 unique symbols/hour on free tier; 1000 requests/hour; 30y+ daily history included
known_quality_issues: Generally very high quality; widely respected in retail quant community
Free tier rate limits are generous for our 3-name × 1 historical fetch use case
pros: Free tier includes full historical depth (no s14-d1 window blocker)
Both adjusted and unadjusted columns in single response (no need to choose convention at fetch — can defer to SEAL)
Free API key registration is fast
vendor_comparison.type: REST API; well-respected data quality reputation

Requires verification of current free-tier endpoint availability before operator commits

cons: Cost; overkill for diagnostic-only research at this scale
